hypothesis: Correcting the aromatic bond valency rounding bug and training generative models on chemically accurate (aromatic-dependent or Kekulized) valency datasets will reduce valency violations and produce generated molecules with lower GFN2-xTB geometry and relaxation energy deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ, ΔE_relax) than models trained on the original flawed dataset, while MMFF94-based evaluation will systematically overestimate relaxation energies relative to GFN2-xTB.
